title: Google Summer of Code 2026
summary: Paid 12-week program for students contributing to open-source projects under mentorship from leading OSS organisations.
eligibility: Students aged 18+, new contributors to open source, any field of study.

title: Rhodes Scholarship 2026
summary: Fully-funded postgraduate study at the University of Oxford for exceptional students worldwide.
eligibility: Ages 18-24, completed undergraduate degree by October 2026, demonstrated leadership.

title: MIT Launch Summer Program 2026
summary: Four-week residential entrepreneurship program where high-school students build real startups on MIT's campus.
eligibility: High school students worldwide, ages 15-18, interested in entrepreneurship.

title: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF)
summary: World's largest pre-college science competition with $9M+ in awards across 22 categories.
eligibility: High school students, grades 9-12, who win a qualifying affiliated science fair.

title: Girls Who Code Summer Immersion Program 2026
summary: Free two-week virtual program teaching computer science to rising 10th-12th grade girls and non-binary students.
eligibility: Rising high school sophomore, junior or senior; girls and non-binary students.

title: Hack the North 2026
summary: Canada's largest hackathon — 36 hours, 1,500+ students, fully-funded travel for international attendees.

title: Coca-Cola Scholars Program 2026
summary: $20,000 achievement-based scholarships for high-school seniors who demonstrate leadership and service.
eligibility: U.S. high school seniors graduating in spring 2026, GPA 3.0+, plan to pursue a bachelor's degree.

title: NASA JPL Summer Internship 2026
summary: 10-week paid summer internship on active NASA missions spanning spacecraft, robotics, and Earth science.
eligibility: U.S. citizens enrolled in accredited university; undergraduate or graduate.

title: Mozilla Technology Fund Fellowship 2026
summary: One-year fellowship supporting open-source projects that advance a healthy, trustworthy internet.
eligibility: Researchers, technologists, and graduate students working on open-internet tools.

title: Climate Tech Fellowship 2026
summary: Two-year fully-funded fellowship for scientists and engineers turning climate research into deployable products.
eligibility: PhD or equivalent research experience in climate-relevant fields.
location: Berkeley, CA / Houston, TX / Boston, MA
